title: vicia tetrasperma subsp. pubescens
intro: Vicia tetrasperma subsp. pubescens, also known as the four-veined vetch or hairy vetch, is a plant species within the legume family that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues and as an expectorant to relieve coughs. The health benefits of Vicia tetrasperma subsp. pubescens include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with bronchitis, as well as its potential antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
